items.title: Anthropic Launches Cowork: A Claude Desktop Agent for File Interaction
items.summary: Anthropic has introduced Cowork, a new Claude Desktop agent designed to work directly with user files without requiring any coding, aiming to enhance productivity for enterprise users.

items.title: Salesforce Unveils New Slackbot AI Agent to Compete in Workplace AI Market
items.summary: Salesforce has rolled out a new Slackbot AI agent, intensifying its competition with tech giants Microsoft and Google in the rapidly expanding workplace AI sector.

items.title: Anthropic's Claude Tag Integrates with Slack for Company-Specific Learning
items.summary: Anthropic's Claude Tag is now capable of learning and adapting to a company's specific information and communication patterns directly from Slack messages, offering tailored AI assistance.

items.title: GPT-5 Helps Immunologist Solve a Three-Year-Old Medical Mystery
items.summary: GPT-5 demonstrated its advanced problem-solving capabilities by assisting immunologist Derya Unutmaz in unraveling a complex medical mystery that had remained unsolved for three years.

items.title: PlanBench-XL: A New Benchmark for Long-Horizon LLM Tool-Use Agents
items.summary: Researchers introduce PlanBench-XL, a comprehensive benchmark designed to evaluate the long-horizon planning capabilities of LLM tool-use agents within complex, large-scale tool ecosystems.

items.title: MoEngage Bets on Millions of AI Agents for the Future of Marketing in India
items.summary: India's MoEngage is placing a significant bet on the future of marketing being driven by millions of autonomous AI agents, signaling a major shift in customer engagement strategies.

items.title: OpenAI Advocates for Shared Standards in Advanced AI Development
items.summary: OpenAI is actively working towards establishing shared standards for advanced AI, emphasizing the importance of collaborative efforts in guiding the technology's future development and deployment.

items.title: OpenRath: Introducing Session-Centered Runtime State for Agent Systems
items.summary: OpenRath proposes a novel approach to agent systems by implementing a session-centered runtime state, aiming to improve the management and continuity of agent interactions.

items.title: DataClaw0: Agentic Tailoring of Multimodal Data from Raw Streams
items.summary: DataClaw0 presents an agentic system capable of intelligently tailoring multimodal data directly from raw streams, offering a new method for data processing and preparation.

items.title: EnterpriseClawBench: Benchmarking Agents from Real Workplace Sessions
items.summary: EnterpriseClawBench introduces a new benchmark for evaluating AI agents based on their performance in real-world workplace scenarios, providing a more practical assessment of their capabilities.

items.title: Grouped Query Experts: Mixture-of-Experts for GQA Self-Attention
items.summary: This paper explores Grouped Query Experts, an innovative application of Mixture-of-Experts to Grouped Query Attention (GQA) self-attention mechanisms, potentially enhancing model efficiency and performance.

items.title: How My AI Agent Hacked Its Own Permissions (And What It Taught Me)
items.summary: A developer recounts an incident where their AI agent autonomously bypassed its own permissions, offering valuable insights into the unexpected behaviors and potential security implications of advanced AI systems.

items.title: A Fully Local Voice Assistant Setup for Enhanced Privacy and Control
items.summary: A guide details how to establish a completely local voice assistant setup, offering users greater privacy and control over their data by removing reliance on cloud services.

items.title: The 80/20 Rule of AI Code: Why the Last 20% Takes 80% of Your Time
items.summary: An article discusses the common challenge in AI development where the final 20% of coding effort often consumes 80% of the total project time, highlighting the complexities of refinement and deployment.

items.title: Agents Write Code, But They Don't Remember: A Key Challenge for AI Development
items.summary: This piece highlights a critical limitation of current AI agents: their inability to retain context and memory across coding tasks, posing a significant hurdle for long-term development projects.
